Um as we start adopting this into your work flow
for anyone who doesn't know already.
Apartment IQ is a online based software.

The way we work is by scraping the publicly facing websites of your subject property as well as your concept properties to pull in all of the publicly available information they have on their availabilities, their price changes.

Um and those units that are available in leasing at their properties so that you can have one concise report that you can edit and manipulate to answer the questions that you have in your day to day as far as uh where apartment IQ gets its data.

Then how often we update uh Our update happens every evening by about eight pm, nine pm eastern time we start scraping so that the next morning when you log in, you have the most up to date information possible as of the prior evening.
So you can always know that your information is at least uh as recent as the prior night
when you log into apartment IQ, you'll most likely uh see this screen here, which will have S two at the top left hand corner and any market surveys that are relevant for you.
I'll go ahead and click in on one at random at this point.
We'll start with Cedar at the branch,
the way we navigate through a market survey, an apartment IQ we have four navigation tabs at the top from left to right.
You can think of this as going from the highest level of information down through different levels of granularity to the most detailed areas in the units and recommendations tab.
When we start here on this property tab, this high level information, uh we're going to be seeing at first these interactive graphs.
I like to call them the leader board of sorts.
What you'll have here is a trailing 12 weeks information on new available units in your market.
And to the right of that, you'll have a leader board that shows you the activity for that market this given week,
we can change this information to look at leased units as well.
Leased units being any units that have come off property websites over the last 12 weeks and then a leader board for the past week.

What's nice is being able to go back and forth between these two and understanding quickly kind of where the market has been from a supply of new units versus the rate at which they're moving off the market.
We can quickly kind of understand, are we at parity?
Uh is there more leasing activity than uh replacement activity?
And what does that mean for our pricing and marketing strategy going forward?
It's a nice visual way to understand your market.

Some high level information broken down here in these columns.
We'll have total units against the total availability.
We'll have a column for new available units as in units that became available within the last 30 days, which will allow you to quickly understand how fast units are coming on at this property.
We also have vacancy which of course is different from availability simply by whether that unit is available today for moving.
We have concession values and details on the concessions that are being listed on a website.
We have recently leased units.
So we have the speed by which new supply is coming on and the speed by which units are moving off as leased.

And then because we can pull concessions, we have a concession value and then an average N er that is calculated, this is by taking the value of the rent concession and dividing that across the 12 month term and subtracting that from the ring

anytime you might have a question about how apartment IQ calculates a given metric using your cursor to hover over some of these metrics will give you a little bit more insight and context as to what this metric is delivering for you.

we can then compare to the average N er per square foot and we're getting a higher dollar value compared to the group and moving units faster.

the rents nominally and the days on market for every unit in the market survey,

Uh We're now looking at specific units and we're saying, hey, what specific units in a given market survey are most relevant to the units that I have available or have recently released?
We're doing this by maintaining a 5 to 10% range between pricing, sizing your beds and your baths as well as a radius, you know, how far apart are these units?

For instance, oftentimes on our pricing calls will be asked, hey, what is the cheapest unit on the market right now?

in our recommendations tab.

If you have a green color code, that means that you are priced under what apartment IQ thinks you could be priced at and would be recommending you to increase your price at a given unit if you're priced in the yellow or the red zone, that means that there's an opportunity where you are priced over the market.
And apartment IQ would recommend you maybe come down.
I always talk about this tab and and highlight that you take this with a grain of salt.

Oftentimes apartment IQ is going to be looking to um look for two different data sources that are saying the same thing to kind of increase our confidence if this is the right piece of information to highlight.
